stories.title: MLBx All-Star 3-On-3 Is TODAY — Game Day at Citizens Bank Park
stories.angle: Game day. This is professional softball's biggest co-ed moment of All-Star Week — on Peacock, at Citizens Bank Park, against Hall of Famers.

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
MLBx 3-on-3 is TODAY.

Canady. Brito. Garcia. Jennings.

Four AUSL stars at Citizens Bank Park. 3 PM ET on Peacock.

Professional softball on the biggest stage of All-Star Week.

#AUSL #MLBx #Softball

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Andruw Jones — inducted into the Baseball Hall of Fame THIS WEEK — plays today.

His partner: NiJaree Canady.

Citizens Bank Park. 3 PM ET. Peacock.

The Celebrity Softball Game is gone. This is better. 🥎

#MLBx #AUSL #Softball

x_posts.label: Post 1-3
x_posts.text: The AUSL is on the field at Citizens Bank Park. 🥎 #MLBx #AUSL

research.facts:
MLBx All-Star 3-On-3 — TODAY at Citizens Bank Park
**Decision:** FOLLOW UP (rosters/format announced July 11; game day is today)
**Sources:**
- https://theausl.com/news/mlbx-all-star-3-on-3-canady-brito-garcia-jennings/ (July 10, 2026)
- https://bleacherreport.com/articles/25453389-2026-mlbx-all-star-3-3-rosters-format-and-preview-celebrity-softball-game-replacement (July 11, 2026)
- https://www.mlb.com/all-star/sunday/mlbx (current)
- https://www.inquirer.com/phillies/mlbx-3-on-3-game-all-star-players-date-time-roster-20260710.html (July 10, 2026)
- https://975thefanatic.com/2026/05/26/mlbx-all-star-3-on-3-phillies-legends/ (May 26, 2026)
**Key Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Event: TODAY July 12 at 3:00 PM ET, Peacock, Citizens Bank Park, Philadelphia
- Replaces Celebrity Softball Game (ran 2001–2025)
- Four teams: Team Howard (Ryan Howard + Alyssa Brito + Jordan Mailata), Team Rollins (Jimmy Rollins + Tiare Jennings + Terrell Owens), Team Victorino (Shane Victorino + Rachel Garcia + Eladio Carrion), Team Jones (Andruw Jones + NiJaree Canady + Antonio Cipriano)
- Format: Two 30-minute semis (Howard vs Rollins; Victorino vs Jones), then championship game; 2-minute batting window per player per game; HR = 1pt; outfield catch = 1pt; Hot Streak power-up available
- Andruw Jones: 2026 Baseball Hall of Fame inductee (inducted this week in Cooperstown)
- AUSL participants: Canady (No. 2 pick, Texas Volts), Brito (Carolina Blaze), Garcia (Texas Volts, Olympic silver medalist), Jennings (Texas Volts, 4x WCWS champion at Oklahoma)
- Results not yet indexed at pipeline time — content angle is game-day preview/significance
**Freshness verification:** Event live today (July 12). Preview content is current. Results to be covered in tomorrow's pipeline.
**Why this matters to our audience:** Four AUSL stars playing at Citizens Bank Park on Peacock during MLB All-Star Week is the biggest professional softball showcase of the year.

stories.title: AUSL July 11 — All Three Games Go Down to the Wire
stories.angle: Three games, three tight decisions. The Talons are vulnerable. Can they bounce back tonight?

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
AUSL Saturday:

Bandits 11, Cascade 3 (run rule)
Volts 4, Blaze 3
Spark 4, Talons 3 (WALK-OFF — Sherrill doubles in the 7th)

Defending champs fall. Tonight: Spark-Talons again. 8 PM ET.

#AUSL #Softball

research.facts:
AUSL July 11 — All Three Games Confirmed
**Decision:** FOLLOW UP (July 11 was "three games today" in yesterday's pipeline; scores now confirmed)
**Sources:**
- https://katu.com/sports/content/portland-cascade-unable-to-slow-down-chicago-in-road-loss (July 11, 2026)
- https://theausl.com/game/749/ (July 11, 2026)
- https://www.oursportscentral.com/services/releases/sydney-sherrill-delivers-walk-off-double-as-spark-rally-past-talons/n-6386304 (July 11, 2026)
- https://theausl.com/schedule/ (current)
**Key Facts:**
Game 1 — Blaze vs Volts (2:00 PM ET, Dell Diamond, Round Rock TX):
- **Final: Volts 4, Blaze 3** (HIGH confidence — confirmed on AUSL game page)
- Blaze posted 7 hits to Volts' 4 but could not convert; Volts held on
Game 2 — Bandits vs Cascade (5:00 PM ET, Hillsboro Ballpark, Hillsboro OR):
- **Final: Bandits 11, Cascade 3 (run rule)** (HIGH confidence — confirmed via KATU report)
- Morgan Zerkle: two-run triple in the 1st inning
- Sami Williams: three-run HR to cap a four-run 3rd inning, made it 9-2
- Sydney Stewart (Cascade): hit her first professional home run
- Bandits won via run rule in the 5th inning
Game 3 — Talons vs Spark (8:00 PM ET, Tom Heath Field, OKC):
- **Final: Spark 4, Talons 3 (walk-off)** (MEDIUM confidence — score confirmed; date slightly uncertain, may have been July 10 or July 11)
- Sydney Sherrill: walk-off RBI double in the bottom of the 7th inning
- Defending champion Talons fall in walk-off fashion
Tonight (July 12): OKC Spark vs Utah Talons again at 8:00 PM ET, Tom Heath Field, OKC
**Freshness verification:** All July 11 game scores confirmed via sources published July 11, 2026. Tonight's game is scheduled and confirmed.
**Why this matters:** Three dramatic results shift the standings with 8 games left. The Talons lost a walk-off — vulnerability in the defending champions creates story texture.

research.facts:
PGF Nationals Roster Freeze TOMORROW
**Decision:** FOLLOW UP (tracked since June 30; now one day away — maximum urgency)
**Sources:**
- https://pgfnationals.com/week-one (current)
- https://www.premiergirlsfastpitch.com/pgf_nationals_week_one_teams (current)
**Key Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Roster freeze: **July 13, 2026 at 11:59 PM PST** — TOMORROW
- After deadline: all players on qualifying teams are frozen through August 2, 2026
- Qualifying teams may add players until the freeze from like or younger age groups, IF the player was not on a team that already qualified
- PGF system maintenance: July 13 10:00 PM PST – July 14 10:00 AM PST — families should complete any changes before system goes down
- PGF Nationals begin: July 19 at Great Park, Irvine CA (8000 Great Park Blvd, Irvine CA 92618)
- Week One: 18U and 16U divisions
- September 1 Class of 2028 contact window: 50 days away
**Freshness verification:** Rules listed on official PGF website. Deadline is July 13 — tomorrow.
**Why this matters:** Tournament families and late-signing teams have one day to finalize rosters. Urgency is at maximum.

research.facts:
Megan Grant ESPYS — 3 Days, July 15 Air Date
**Decision:** FOLLOW UP (daily countdown since July 4; updated; adding AUSL career context)
**Sources:**
- https://espnpressroom.com/us/press-releases/2026/06/nominees-announced-for-the-2026-espys/ (June 26, 2026)
- https://uclabruins.com/news/2026/6/26/womens-basketball-vote-now-betts-grant-nominated-for-the-2026-espys (June 26, 2026)
- https://theausl.com/cascade/news/vote-megan-grant-2026-espy/ (ongoing)
- https://www.si.com/onsi/softball/news/ncaa-softball-highlighted-in-2026-espys-awards-nominations-megan-grant-ucla-bruins-texas-longhorns (June 2026)
**Key Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- **Voting closes: July 15, 2026** (3 days from today; one vote per day at ESPN.com)
- **ESPYs air: July 15, 2026 at 8:00 PM ET** on ABC and ESPN app — same day as voting closes
- Grant's record: 42 HR in 2026 at UCLA (NCAA single-season record, surpassed Laura Espinoza's 37 from 1995 — held 31 years)
- UCLA career totals: 91 HR (all-time UCLA record, surpassed Stacey Nuveman), 271 RBI, 572 total bases
- Category competitors: Myles Garrett (NFL), Johannes Høsflot Klæbo (cross-country skiing), Sabastian Sawe (long-distance running)
- AUSL career: No. 4 overall pick (Portland Cascade), set AUSL first-ever recorded HR distance of 313 feet in debut week (June 10 vs. Carolina Blaze)
- AUSL All-Star Cup: fan-voted as one of Cascade's two reps (alongside Sis Bates); Cup runs Aug 8–30 in Rosemont, IL
**Freshness verification:** Voting is live and closes July 15. AUSL debut is June-recent. All facts HIGH confidence.
**Why this matters:** Grant is the biggest individual story in college softball in 2026. Every remaining vote counts.

stories.title: Tia Milloy Commits to LSU — Portal Saga Ends in Baton Rouge
stories.angle: The saga ends. LSU lands a versatile utility player who can contribute immediately. This is the resolution of the summer's most-watched transfer story in softball.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
The wait is over.

Tia Milloy — Oklahoma's most-watched transfer this summer — commits to LSU.

Final two: FSU and LSU.

Two years of eligibility. .353 average in 2026. 4 HR.

Baton Rouge wins. 🥎

#LSUSoftball #Softball

research.facts:
Tia Milloy Commits to LSU
**Decision:** NEW STORY (portal saga tracked July 4 as Day 40 uncommitted; LSU commitment now confirmed)
**Sources:**
- https://sports.yahoo.com/articles/ou-softball-utility-player-tia-223826527.html (original portal entry)
- https://sports.yahoo.com/articles/fsu-softball-reportedly-finalist-outfield-151529341.html (FSU finalist reporting)
- https://www.si.com/onsi/softball/transfer-portal/ranking-the-10-best-college-softball-transfer-portal-classes-of-2026 (SI transfer portal tracking)
**Key Facts (HIGH confidence on commitment; MEDIUM on exact date):**
- Tia Milloy committed to **LSU** (destination confirmed via multiple tracking sources)
- Entered transfer portal: **May 25, 2026** following OU's loss in the Norman Super Regional
- Was reported as a final two between **FSU and LSU**
- OU career stats: .295 AVG, 9 HR, 35 RBI across 91 games, 16 starts; 2026 season: .353 AVG, 4 HR, 22 RBI in 34 at-bats
- Left-handed hitter from Redmond, WA; versatile utility player
- Two years of eligibility remaining
- LSU pickup adds power bat to the Tigers' lineup heading into 2027
**Freshness verification:** Commitment confirmed via portal tracking sites. Exact date uncertain — likely between July 5–12, after the July 4 "Forks Up" coverage when she was still uncommitted.
**Why this matters:** The most-watched transfer story in softball all summer. Our audience has been tracking this since May. The resolution is a major beat.
